description: Apollo 9 Flight Journal: Day 1: Orbit 1 - Earth Orbit

tokens: And, Apollo 9, you are go in the orbit.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And your CMC is go; it is valid.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And, Apollo 9, the S-IVB has been safed.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Do you have our apogee and perigee?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Apollo 9, the S-IVB has been configured for orbit.
It's looking real good, and your SPS helium is solid as a rock.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We switched over all right, I guess, and everything looks good.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: What kind of orbit did you get us in?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We don't have it yet, Apollo 9.
We are still running it through the computers.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Roger, with our S-band radar we're showing you 107 [nautical miles, 198 km] by 98.9 [nautical miles, 183 km] as the first cut.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And we are continuing to massage this, Apollo 9, and we will keep you updated.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We've got 1 minute with you at Canaries, and we will see you over Tananarive at 37.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: It might help, Dave, I don't know.
Dave, do you want the floodlights on?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Do you want my floodlights on?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, why don't you turn them on?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, the sun's still shining on my side, Rusty.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, they're not bad through the telescope, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let's get the first one in - Sirius.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can see the stars now.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Well, we're pitching around pretty fast.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through Tananarive.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Our Canary data shows your orbit at 103.9 [nautical miles, 192.4 km] by 102.3 [nautical miles, 189.4 km].
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: That is affirmative, and that changes slightly as the S-IVB vents, but that was a pretty good hack at it on Canary.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: That's not a bad boost, then.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: No, we had 88 [nautical miles, 163 km] by 138 [nautical miles, 256 km].
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: And we'll have you here at Tananarive for about another 5 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: You got the - you say that our SPS helium tank pressure looks good, huh?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: David says his first star angle is five balls.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Did you copy the torquing angles?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We have no data here at Tananarive, Dave.
You'll have to read them to me.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I copy the time and the angles.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Roger, looks like the platform was right there.
And that was a nice speedy job on that 52.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Hey, I've got the Moon out here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Jettison the dust covers; I think if we were flying over some storm now, I see some lightning out there.
Jettison the dust covers; backup comm check.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: No, I haven't done that backup comm check --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: And (garbled) purge checks, caution and warning system, SCS attitude reference comparison.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: That Moon really wipes out the star field.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: These pencils are going to really be a bear.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: The one I have doesn't have any Velcro on it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: That's because you've been using it so much.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: No, there's no knob - nub on it.
Okay, we've got the - I'm going to go to the flight plan, here in a minute.
I see I made a terrible mistake.
I should have brought the whole mini-flight plan with me.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I - I got mine, Jim, if you need one.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I have the first day, but I should have brought the rest of it, too.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, we got to prepare the cameras - the Hasselblad and the sequence camera.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'll unstow the checklist and the cameras.
We don't need the checklist; we've got them - GDC align --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We are going to lose you here at Tananarive in about 45 seconds, and we'll see you over Carnarvon at 52.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, first star check on Regor.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Regor has a little (garbled) on it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You wouldn't recognize Regor if you saw it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, listen, Regor has a little tiny star right next to it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll tell you, I wouldn't recognize Regor if (garbled)
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, Dave, are you in 4 Bravo down there?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: No, 4 Bravo - What do you mean?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, okay, that's because the main buses - the battery buses are low.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Think you're sorry, we wasted all that time (garbled)?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'm just so happy to be disappointed.
Okay, I got the ECS monitoring check done, and everything looks good there.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Rusty, do you want to turn the cabin fan off (garbled)?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, I'll try them both off.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'll probably find out that my green handles don't (garbled)
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Did you get the GDC alignments all done, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I got the GDC alignments done.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I got the service module RCS check done, the command module RCS done, the EMS check done.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Service module RCS monitoring - You got that?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Jet - jettison the dust covers - You got that done.
I'm waiting for the backup comm check and 52 - P52.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: (garbled) the flight plan (garbled) out, Dave, and see (garbled)
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: What the hell am I going to do with an update book.
The problem is the whole - the whole (garbled) and we don't even (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Let me - I'll just stick it temporarily.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Now, I guess you could do your SPS attitude reference (garbled) DSKY.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, the fuel cell purge check; I'm just going to check O, right?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, all we're supposed to do is check it and see if it doesn't leak anymore.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, fuel cell 1 purge is okay; fuel cell 2 is - very good.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Stand by for Master Alarm - no sweat Master Alarm.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, caution and warning lamp test, on.
How many are we supposed to have out?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: One, two, three, four - all on the bottom row.
One, two, three, four are out.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Did you get your - your Master Alarm, Jim, up there?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That looks good - I got that.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I forgot to watch the altimeter during the launch to see if it works.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, caution and warning - checked that.
Hey, you installing the optics there, Davey?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: (garbled) You got the SCS AUTO reference comparison, Jim?
Or are we going to do that?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'll do that in just a second.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, and I'm going to go on with the SPS monitor checks.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Anyone remember where we put the checklist?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, why don't you give me the bag, so you won't have to sit there and hold it?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Oh, this is a heck of a lot harder to operate than the other one.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Well, you just can't put all these (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, you just - can't sit on anything (garbled)
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Reading you loud and clear through Carnarvon.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I'm presently in a backup comm check - step five there on LMP 1-2, and I'm on line 5.
I got the initial contact, and I got my S-band volume up.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Roger, Understand you are in step 5 and stand by one here.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And I'm standing by for a go for the backup voice check.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We will give you a go on that in about 30 seconds here.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We are standing by for your voice check on the S-band.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I got one blast in there sounded like you keyed, and that was all.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston on the VHF.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston on - via the VHF.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I gave you a call on Downvoice Backup, and evidently you are not reading on it.
However, I'm reading you up on the S-band.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, and we confirmed with the site that we did not get an S-band downlink on that one, Rusty.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We will be standing by for suggestions.
Let me just give you my configuration here, if you want to copy that.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'm on the primary transponder, and I'm reading you okay - up okay.
Everything else is in Normal there.
Going across, I've got the ranging switch off, I've got the S-band Aux Tape in Downvoice Backup.
I've got the power PMP back up to Normal, and everything else is vanilla.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We are going to have you here about another minute at Carnarvon, and then we are going to pick up over Honeysuckle at about 50 - it'll be just about on the hour, so have your S-band volumes up at that time.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And be advised, we are rushing on through all our checklist here, and we've got most everything done.
The fuel cell purge check checked out okay.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Roger, Sounds great and, Apollo 9, you are go for 6-4.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And, Houston, be advised that I'm going to go out of this backup comm check configuration here and go back to Normal.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Roger, Let's meet you over Honeysuckle in normal configuration just about on the hour.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through Honeysuckle.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through Honeysuckle.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: You're 5-square on S-band, Apollo - or Houston.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You're coming in, and if you want to try this backup comm check again, we can support it.
And just as we were leaving Carnarvon, the downlink appeared to be coming through on the backup.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Why don't we forego it right now, and we'll try to check that at some quiet period.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Here, let me see if that (garbled) gauge - No, NA and NB look okay, and it's just the SPS pressure that's down.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, that sounds like we've got a good (garbled) bad news if (garbled)
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, that would have been bad news, we'd really (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, I guess there's no sense in monitoring that thing.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (garbled) I don't know whether it was the (garbled) coming off or what it was.
I think it came out of that hole up there.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Here goes one - (garbled) and I already have it.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, you know you can see the Earth real good now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me turn my floods down (garbled) Hey, we're coming back into some daylight.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Dave, that backup - that data is on the back of that sheet.
Yes, you see how to write it into the block update stuff here?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, let's do the PIPA bias (garbled)
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, you want to store the COAS (garbled) Yes, you've got to get the ORDEAL and (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We are going to lose you here at Honeysuckle in about 40 seconds, and we will see you over Huntsville in about 3 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Did you get the fuel cell purge checked, Rusty?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You did the SPS monitor check which (garbled)?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I got to find some stupid place to put my helmet now.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: That's the end of the checklist as far as I can see.
We haven't done the PIPA bias check.
You've done the P52, and that's good.
You've done your backup - No, you haven't done that yet.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: That's not a very good place to - Yes, it is.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Rusty, did you get the ECS monitor check?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: How about the water gun - is that working?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: And, Apollo 9, this is Houston through the Huntsville.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Let's see, Dave, I've got to help you (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Huntsville cannot maintain valid two-way range, so we lost signal bearing in advancing.
transcript.speaker: Unknown

tokens: You sure get a lot of - a lot of gas in that.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Huntsville is valid in two way (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Unknown

tokens: And, Apollo 9, this is Houston through the Huntsville.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: You're coming through weak, but clear.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We don't have much to pass you here - we're only going to have you for about another minute and a half, and we'll talk to you as you come across the States and pass the data to you then.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I'm trying to find a checklist, huh?
Yes, well, here's (garbled) it's got the flight plan, procedures book, and crew log (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: And, Apollo 9, this is Houston if you can read me.
We'll see you over the Redstone at about 24.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Boy, the sun's come up like thunder over Galway Bay, or something.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Man, oh man, it's daytime again.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Let's see how the windows are doing.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Well, Dave, it's supposed to be in the data card - cloth bag.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'll be damned if I can't get my temporary stowage bag open for anything!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, hey, let's get these Mae Wests off before we (garbled) down.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: As a matter of fact, I'll even stow (garbled)
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, you - you're pretty safe until about -
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll get my - Cabin pressure's now 5.4 [psi, 37.2 kPa] by the way.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: That sounds like a McDivitt talking.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No, I haven't done it yet.
I'm going to save mine up.
When I go, buddy, you're going to know it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, and I'm going to get some (garbled)
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We should get one out of (garbled) set.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Well, at least that's why (garbled) for me.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's hard as heck to get this thing out of these (garbled), isn't it, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, I'm going to lose all this stuff (garbled) I've got some time.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Dave, if you got those cameras all (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Would you believe it's getting very difficult to see the numbers over there on the EMS Delta-V counter?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Dave, you got to move it down (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, you're able to move around a little bit.
I don't have to worry about sticking my feet through the instrument panel.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Dave, you want to take your Mae West off?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, I'm going to put the lights (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Dave, you got another - another clip?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Well, maybe we ought to just get them all out, Dave.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, let's just get them all out.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Photo (garbled) plan - photo (garbled) plan, it says on day 1.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, if somebody spits, it (garbled) right on (garbled) stuff.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: (garbled) a roll of tape out (garbled) and stick it up here where (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, I wonder if we ought to tape up our pens.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, tape up our spare pens, so it doesn't evaporate, dry out.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Boy, we've been up here an hour already.
(garbled) Post-insertion unstowage, mount and initialize ORDEAL, SPS monitor check - Shoot, we're way the hell ahead of schedule.
Extend the docking probe (garbled) The next thing that I can see (garbled) that's at 41 - 48 or so.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Maybe you ought to get the big flight plan.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, is the tape recorder on, Mr.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Well, I wanted to do that - debriefing on tape.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'll get the little one for you.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: What else do we need - a camera.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: (garbled) The most important thing we need right now is a camera (garbled) so that we have them -
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: (garbled) Hasselblad, and that's got Mag A on it --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Turn the tape recorder on (garbled) During the boost, lift-off was noticeable (garbled) vibrations (garbled) vibrations 1 or 2 seconds.

At T minus - T plus 12 minutes, (garbled) roll was noticeable and giving oscillations on the ratemeter of about 1 degree per second (garbled) vibrations and oscillations (garbled), and no problems during the (garbled) --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through the Redstone.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: It was very quiet up until about 50 seconds and (garbled) high peak (garbled) vibrated (garbled) not bad at all (garbled) quiet out again about (garbled) --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through the Redstone.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: You're clear as a bell, Apollo 9; this is Houston.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And, Apollo 9, we'd like to confirm that you are in Omni Baker and primary S-band transponder.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Let me do that for you.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: It may be a coincidence, but we lost data just about the time I gave you that transmission to clarify that Omni Baker.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We are - we were on Delta and I just switched it to Omni [Baker] for you.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I understand you did go from Delta to Baker and the primary transponder was on.
You didn't need to change that, did you?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: How are you doing down there, Smokey?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: And we're - you can anticipate we'll probably have a state vector we want to uplink over Bermuda or Vanguard - 5 or 10 minutes, and for Rusty's benefit, the backup comm check over Carnarvon was 5-square.
It came in - we had a momentary dropout there, but we got it real good.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We'll write that one off then.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We have got all of the checklist done except the glycol and some things that we're going to do right now.
And we haven't taken the PIPA bias check either.
I guess you guys want to do that.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We have no data right now, 9.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: For your info we do have our data coming in now solid.
And Jim - for the bias check - we really will get a good one on you after TD&E.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We just got a Master Alarm cryo press here on the number 1 H tank.
It's just off the lower limit here.
You might want to take a look at that.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We'll see what we can do for you.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, and the heater just came on, and it's going back up again.
It looks like it's just tickling the Master Alarm there before it decides to heat up.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That's probably SimSup just playing with the tolerances a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Roosa
